## Worth mentioning

### 1. Anthropic's Claude models went offline due to US government policy clash over Fable jailbreak

- Source: [simonwillison.net](https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/15/axios-clashes-anthropics/#atom-everything)
- Category: Model + API Changes
- Published: 2026-06-17T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: early_signal, publish_public
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- Anthropic's Claude models were taken offline due to conflict with the US government over the Fable 5 jailbreak and export control classification.

Builder/operator takeaway: Direct impact on Claude API availability — affects any builder running Claude-dependent automation or agents.

Uncertainty: Based on anonymous sources cited in Axios; Anthropic has not made official statements. Exact timeline and restoration status unclear.

### 2. Vercel Functions can now run up to 30 minutes

- Source: [vercel.com](https://vercel.com/changelog/vercel-functions-can-now-run-up-to-30-minutes)
- Category: Model + API Changes
- Published: 2026-06-17T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: vendor_claim, publish_private
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- Vercel Pro/Enterprise Functions can now run up to 30 minutes for Node.js and Python runtimes, up from the previous 800-second limit.

Builder/operator takeaway: Changes the calculus for long-running tasks on Vercel — previously you'd need a queue/worker setup to stay under the old limit.

Uncertainty: Pro/Enterprise only; other runtimes not yet supported.

### 3. datasette-agent 0.3a0: execute_write_sql with user approval

- Source: [simonwillison.net](https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/15/datasette-agent/#atom-everything)
- Category: AI Operations / Agent Control
- Published: 2026-06-17T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: reproducible, publish_private
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- datasette-agent 0.3a0 adds execute_write_sql tool that requests user approval before writing to the database, implementing human-in-the-loop for agent DB writes.

Builder/operator takeaway: Human approval pattern for agent DB writes is directly applicable to MCP server design for agentic workflows.

Uncertainty: Alpha release; API may change.

### 4. A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer

- Source: [roman.pt](https://roman.pt/posts/linkedin-backdoor/)
- Category: AI Operations / Agent Control
- Published: 2026-06-17T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: reproducible, publish_public
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- A security researcher documented a supply chain attack where a LinkedIn job offer directed developers to clone a GitHub repo that installed a persistent backdoor.

Builder/operator takeaway: Real documented attack vector targeting developers via LinkedIn — high risk for solo devs who review code for freelance or contract work.

Uncertainty: Specifics of the backdoor mechanism depend on reading the full post.

### 5. Cloudflare CAPTCHA triggered by URLs containing ampersands

- Source: [simonwillison.net](https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/16/captcha-on-at-least-one-ampersand/#atom-everything)
- Category: Data Infrastructure / Verification / Scraping
- Published: 2026-06-17T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: builder_reported, publish_private
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- Cloudflare WAF Managed Challenge rules can trigger CAPTCHA on URLs containing literal ampersands, breaking faceted search engines and similar query-string-heavy pages.

Builder/operator takeaway: Practical gotcha that would be hard to debug without knowing the cause — saves hours of debugging time.

Uncertainty: May depend on specific WAF rule configuration; not necessarily universal across all Cloudflare setups.

## Monitor (keep an eye on it)

### 7. Iroh 1.0: Dial Keys, not IPs

- Source: [iroh.computer](https://www.iroh.computer/blog/v1)
- Category: Tools Worth Testing
- Published: 2026-06-17T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: reproducible, monitor
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- Iroh 1.0 released — a stable Rust P2P networking library that uses public keys instead of IPs for connections, with NAT traversal and relay fallback.

Builder/operator takeaway: Stable 1.0 P2P library for direct device-to-device connections without centralized infrastructure.

Uncertainty: Rust-only for now; ecosystem adoption unclear.
